keyRules: Maximum height: 120 m (400 ft) above ground level
Drones weighing more than 250g: mandatory registration with SISANT (ANAC)
Drones weighing up to 250g: exempt from registration, automatically licensed
Minimum distance of 5.4 km from airports for low-altitude flights
Minimum distance of 9 km from airports for flights up to 120 m
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
A minimum distance of 30 meters from people not involved in the operation
SARPAS (DECEA) authorization is required before each flight in controlled airspace
Transmission equipment must be certified by ANATEL
Night flights require specific authorization and anti-collision lighting
importantNotes: Tourists: Brazil ALLOWS recreational flights, but drones weighing more than 250g must be registered with SISANT before the flight
Fines imposed by ANAC can reach R$ 7,000 per violation — operating without registration or SARPAS authorization is a serious offense
National parks (ICMBio) and indigenous areas (FUNAI) generally prohibit drones without specific authorization
Beaches and urban tourist areas (Rio, São Paulo, Salvador) have municipal restrictions—check with local authorities
Regulatory change expected in 2026: RBAC No. 100 will replace RBAC-E No. 94 — public consultation concluded in July 2025

primaryLinks.title: ANAC — Drones (Rules and Registration)
primaryLinks.description: Official website of the National Civil Aviation Agency, featuring all the rules, categories, and requirements for drones

primaryLinks.title: SISANT — Drone Registration (ANAC)
primaryLinks.description: Mandatory registration system for drones weighing more than 250g. Register as PR (recreational) or PP (professional)

primaryLinks.title: SARPAS — Flight Authorization (DECEA)
primaryLinks.description: DECEA system for requesting flight authorization in controlled airspace. Required before every flight

primaryLinks.title: ANATEL — Equipment Certification
primaryLinks.description: Check whether your drone's transmitter is certified by ANATEL for use in Brazil

secondaryLinks.title: AISWEB — NOTAMs and aeronautical charts (DECEA)
secondaryLinks.description: Active NOTAMs and official DECEA aeronautical charts to check for temporary restrictions

secondaryLinks.title: ANAC — Frequently Asked Questions About Drones
secondaryLinks.description: ANAC's Official Responses to Common Questions from Drone Operators

secondaryLinks.title: RBAC-E No. 94 — Complete Technical Standard
secondaryLinks.description: Special Brazilian Civil Aviation Regulation No. 94 — full text applicable to drones

fields.disclaimerText: Always consult the official sources from ANAC and DECEA before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Brazil enforces RBAC-E No. 94 (currently transitioning to RBAC No. 100), which requires mandatory approval through SARPAS.
